summary: Comprehensive backend + frontend testing of Nexus Network landing page, Stripe checkout, admin panel and Polish content. Backend: 25/26 pytest passed (96%). Frontend: All key flows verified (hero, packages tabs, buy dialog, Stripe redirect, contact, FAQ, admin login, admin CRUD UI, transactions). One backend bug found in /api/checkout/status/{session_id} returning 500 due to Stripe session-not-found in emergent proxy.

critical.issue: Returns 500 (CheckoutError: 'No such checkout.session') even for session_ids that were JUST created via POST /api/checkout/create. The emergentintegrations Stripe proxy returns 404 on retrieve for sessions it created seconds earlier. This breaks the /platnosc/sukces page which calls this endpoint to confirm payment. Reproduction: 1) POST /api/checkout/create with valid package_id -> get session_id, 2) immediately GET /api/checkout/status/{session_id} -> 500. Backend log: 'stripe._error.InvalidRequestError: No such checkout.session: cs_test_...'. Likely cause: the emergent Stripe proxy stores sessions in a different namespace than what stripe.checkout.Session.retrieve uses, OR session propagation delay. Mitigation: catch InvalidRequestError and either retry, or fall back to returning the locally stored transaction state from payment_transactions collection.

ui_bugs.issue: The huge gradient 'NETWORK' word in the hero is right-clipped on 1920px viewport — the right edge of the last 'K' is cut off because the text overflows past the visible area while a side info-card column sits on the right. Visible in /tmp/landing_hero.png. Consider reducing font-size at lg breakpoints or adding right padding to the text block.

Sonner success toast 'Zalogowano' overlaps the 'Wyloguj' button in the top-right immediately after login (toast position: top-right, button position: top-right). Toast is transient (~3s) but obstructs logout while visible.

integration_issues.issue: Will be broken end-to-end because it depends on GET /api/checkout/status/{session_id} which returns 500 (see backend critical issue). Frontend payment success confirmation cannot work until backend status endpoint is fixed.

action_items: Fix GET /api/checkout/status/{session_id}: wrap stripe_checkout.get_checkout_status in try/except InvalidRequestError; on session-not-found, return current DB-stored payment_transactions state instead of 500. This is required for the /platnosc/sukces page to work.
(Low) Adjust hero 'NETWORK' typography or layout so the gradient text is not clipped on 1920px viewport.
(Low) Move the success toast off the top-right or shift admin header buttons to avoid overlap with toast.
critical_code_review_comments: Backend checkout_status updates the DB only after a successful Stripe retrieve, so a Stripe API hiccup leaves the transaction stuck at 'initiated/pending' forever AND throws 500 to the user. Should degrade gracefully.
Backend uses deprecated @app.on_event('startup'/'shutdown') (FastAPI deprecation). Recommend migrating to lifespan context manager.

Admin password is auto-reset to env value on every backend startup if admin exists — good for testing, but in production this means password changes via env-var rotate without audit. Document this behavior.
Stripe currency is hardcoded to 'pln' server-side and amount is read from DB package — correctly prevents frontend price manipulation. Good.
All interactive elements have data-testid attributes (testids on Contact form use '-input' suffix; on AdminLogin use '-submit' suffix — naming is inconsistent across components but functional).
seed_data_creation: Backend auto-seeds 10 packages + admin on startup. Verified 4 hosting (Basic 50, Normal 60, Pro 70 featured, VIP 100), 3 addons, 3 maps — all prices in PLN as expected.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py covers all endpoints. Only failing test: TestCheckout::test_checkout_creates_session — the create call succeeds and returns Stripe URL, but the subsequent GET /api/checkout/status/{session_id} returns 500 from the emergent Stripe proxy ('No such checkout.session'). After main agent fixes the status endpoint (graceful fallback), re-run the suite. Frontend works fully; PaymentSuccess page was not exercised in this iteration because checkout redirected to real Stripe — only the create+redirect path was validated. Admin testids: admin-login-{email,password,submit}, admin-{tab-packages,tab-transactions,new-package-button,edit-{id},delete-{id}}, admin-form-{category,name,price,old-price,promo,sort,description,specs,featured,active,cancel,save}. Contact form testids use '-input' / '-button' suffixes (contact-name-input, contact-email-input, contact-subject-input, contact-message-input, contact-submit-button).
rca of the issue: POST /api/checkout/create -> calls integrations.emergentagent.com/stripe/v1/checkout/sessions and gets a 200 with valid cs_test_... session id and pay URL. Immediately after, GET /api/checkout/status/{session_id} -> emergentintegrations library calls stripe.checkout.Session.retrieve(session_id) which goes back to integrations.emergentagent.com but the proxy responds 404 'No such checkout.session'. The library raises CheckoutError, server raises 500. The session DOES exist (URL works in browser, reaches Stripe checkout). Therefore the proxy's GET endpoint is inconsistent with its POST endpoint, OR the library is using two different api modes. Mitigation in our code: in checkout_status handler, catch the exception, log it, and return CheckoutStatusOut based on the locally persisted payment_transactions document (we already have status/payment_status/amount/currency/package_name there). This makes the success page resilient and still updateable later via the /api/webhook/stripe handler.
